story.title: Our Story
paragraphs: [NAZIV FIRME] is a family company dedicated to providing dignified and professional funeral services, with a special focus on international repatriation of the deceased.
For years, we have helped families in Serbia and abroad to organise the transport of their loved one with respect, discretion and meticulous attention to detail.
We understand how difficult this moment is. That is why every step is taken with empathy, clarity and transparency, so you have support you can rely on.

kako-organizovati-prevoz-pokojnika-iz-in.title: How to Organise Repatriation of the Deceased from Abroad?
kako-organizovati-prevoz-pokojnika-iz-in.excerpt: Organising international repatriation of the deceased may seem complicated, but with expert help the entire process can proceed quickly and with dignity.
content: Losing a loved one abroad brings many challenges, both emotional and administrative. The first step is to contact a professional service that will explain the procedure and help with next steps.
After gathering basic information, our team takes over communication with institutions, consulates and other authorities. The necessary documentation is prepared, and transport is then scheduled.

The time required for organisation depends on the country where the deceased is located and how quickly permits are issued. In the majority of cases, the entire process can be completed within a few days.

koja-dokumentacija-je-potrebna-za-medjun.title: What Documentation Is Required for International Repatriation?
koja-dokumentacija-je-potrebna-za-medjun.excerpt: Several key documents are required for international repatriation of the deceased. Here is what is needed and how we help arrange them.
content: The basic documents include a medical certificate of cause of death, the deceased's passport, and a transport permit issued by the authorities of the country where death occurred.
In addition, document translations, certifications, and burial or cremation certificates are often required. Our team handles all the paperwork and ensures every document is in order.
The family usually only needs to provide the deceased's personal documents and their own details. We take care of everything else.

sta-uraditi-u-sucaju-smrti-u-inostranstv.title: What to Do in the Event of a Death Abroad?
sta-uraditi-u-sucaju-smrti-u-inostranstv.excerpt: The death of a loved one abroad is an extremely difficult moment. Find out what the first steps are and how to get expert help.
content: The first thing to do is contact local emergency services and police in the country where the incident occurred. After that, the nearest consular service of the Republic of Serbia should be notified.
The consular service can help with information and the necessary documents. At the same time, you can contact a specialised company for international repatriation of the deceased.
Our team is available 24/7 and can provide guidance, organise transport and handle all administration.

troskovi-medjunarodnog-prevoza-pokojnika.title: Costs of International Repatriation of the Deceased
troskovi-medjunarodnog-prevoza-pokojnika.excerpt: The cost of international repatriation depends on several factors. We explain what affects the total amount and how to get an accurate estimate.
content: The main factors affecting the price are the distance between countries, the type of transport, documentation, customs and consular fees, as well as any additional family requests.
Every situation is specific, so we recommend contacting us for an individual estimate. Based on basic information we can give you an approximate price within a few hours.
We strive to be transparent and explain all cost items to the family before organisation begins.

kako-izabrati-pogrebno-preduzece.title: How to Choose a Funeral Company?
kako-izabrati-pogrebno-preduzece.excerpt: Choosing a reliable funeral company is an important decision. Here is what to pay attention to when selecting one.
content: First of all, check the company's experience and references. It is important that the company has experience in international repatriation and administration if you need it.
Also, pay attention to availability – a reliable company should be available 24/7, especially in emergencies.
Finally, choose a company that provides clear communication, an empathetic approach and transparency in its operations.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Answers to the most common questions about our services
faq.description: If you have further questions, please feel free to contact us. We are here to help you.

availability.question: Are you available 24/7?
availability.answer: Yes, our services are available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week, including weekends and holidays. You can always call us in case of urgency.

countries.question: From which countries do you organise transport?
countries.answer: We organise transport from all European countries, including Germany, Austria, Switzerland, Italy, France, Belgium, the Netherlands, Sweden, Norway and many others.

documentation.question: Do you handle documentation arrangements?
documentation.answer: Yes, we handle all administration, including communication with consulates, translations, certifications and collecting all necessary permits.

duration.question: How long does it take to organise transport?
duration.answer: The time depends on the country and the speed of permit issuance, but in most cases transport can be organised within 2 to 5 working days.

costs.question: How can I get a cost estimate?
costs.answer: You can call us or fill in the contact form. Based on the country, city and basic information, you will receive a free and non-binding estimate.

preparation.question: What does the family need to prepare?
preparation.answer: The family usually only needs to provide the deceased's personal documents and their contact details. We take care of all other documentation and logistics.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: [NAZIV FIRME] is committed to protecting the privacy of all website users and clients. This policy explains how we collect, use and store your personal data.

data.title: What data we collect
data.content: We collect only the data necessary to provide our services: full name, contact phone, email address, country and city from which transport is organised, as well as the content of your message.

use.title: How we use your data
use.content: We use your data solely for communication, preparing a quote and organising the services you have requested. We do not share your data with third parties without your consent.

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.content: The website may use essential cookies necessary for functionality and analytics. You can control cookies in your browser settings.

rights.title: Your rights
rights.content: You have the right to request access to, correction or deletion of your personal data. Contact us by email or phone.

terms.intro: By using the [NAZIV FIRME] website, you accept the following terms. Please read this page carefully.

general.title: General Provisions
general.content: The content of the website is for informational purposes. We make every effort to ensure the accuracy and currency of information, but we cannot guarantee completeness at all times.

services.title: Services
services.content: All services are provided on the basis of a separate agreement with the family. Service costs depend on the country, distance, documentation and additional requirements.

liability.title: Limitation of Liability
liability.content: [NAZIV FIRME] is liable for actions within its authority and legal obligations. We are not liable for delays caused by unforeseeable circumstances or incomplete information provided to us.

contact.title: Contact
contact.content: For any questions regarding the terms of use, please contact us via the contact page.

intro.title: Professional Transport Organisation
paragraphs: International repatriation of the deceased requires expertise, attention to detail and compliance with the legal regulations of multiple countries. Our team has many years of experience in organising these transports.

We provide complete support to families, from the first contact to completion of the service. We take over communication with institutions, consulates and other authorities, so you can focus on what matters most.

documentation.title: Required Documentation
documentation.intro: A certain amount of documentation is required for international repatriation of the deceased. We help you at every step.
items: Medical certificate of cause of death
Passport or ID card of the deceased
Transport permit issued by the authorities
Certificates from consulates and other institutions

cta.title: Do you need help with transport?
cta.subtitle: Contact us 24/7. We are here to help you.

hero.title: Documentation and Administration
hero.subtitle: We handle all paperwork and communication with institutions, so you have one less worry.

whatWeHandle.title: What Documentation We Handle
paragraphs: International repatriation of the deceased requires extensive documentation, including medical certificates, permits, passports, translations and certifications. Our team has experience in handling all these documents.
We communicate with local authorities, consulates, hospitals, cemeteries and other institutions at home and abroad. Our goal is to make the process as fast and stress-free as possible for the family.
items: Collection of medical certificates and reports
Preparation of customs and transport documentation

procedure.title: How the Procedure Works
paragraphs: After the first contact, we analyse the situation and determine which documents are needed in the specific case. We then take over communication with all relevant authorities.
We keep the family regularly informed of progress and resolve any uncertainties immediately. Once the documentation is ready, we schedule transport.

whyHelp.title: Why Expert Help Is Important
paragraphs: Incorrect or incomplete documentation can lead to delays in transport and additional costs. That is why it is important to have an experienced partner who knows the procedure.
Our team is familiar with the regulations of different countries and knows how to efficiently resolve administrative challenges.

family.title: What the Family Needs to Prepare
paragraphs: The family usually only needs to provide basic details about the deceased, such as first name, surname, date of birth, nationality and passport or ID number.
In some cases a power of attorney or proof of kinship is also required. We provide detailed instructions on what is specifically needed.

cta.title: Send us an inquiry for help with documentation
cta.subtitle: We are here to take over all administration on your behalf.
